---

## Components
- **Page title**: Large, bold, left-aligned — functions as a section header, not a nav bar
- **Tab bar (filter)**: Horizontal scrollable tabs with underline indicator on active state; includes a "+" icon at the far left for adding custom tabs
- **"New" badge**: Small green pill label on the "Featured" tab — inline with tab label, not superscript
- **Article row**: Avatar + author name header, bold title, subtitle/deck text, right-aligned thumbnail, metadata footer
- **Metadata row**: Star/boost icon (gold), date, clap count with hand icon, comment count with bubble icon — all small, muted gray
- **Action buttons**: Minus/block icon (circle outline) and ellipsis (more options) — subtle, low-weight
- **FAB**: Green circular floating action button with edit/compose icon, bottom-right
- **Bottom nav**: Icon-only, minimal; profile icon has a small gold sparkle indicator (notification or new content signal)

---

## Typography & Color
Strong two-level hierarchy: bold serif-style headline at ~18–20pt, regular body/deck text at ~14pt in medium gray. Author/publication attribution is small and uses mixed weight (publication name bold, author name regular). Metadata is smallest, lightest — clearly tertiary. 

Color palette is near-monochrome: white background, black headlines, gray body and metadata. Two accent colors used sparingly and functionally — **gold/yellow** for the boost star icon and FAB sparkle badge, **green** for the "New" pill badge and FAB button. These two accent colors carry all the interactive/status signaling.

---

## Patterns
- **Open card layout with dividers instead of boxed cards** — feels editorial, like a newspaper, rather than app-like. Reduces visual noise significantly.
- **"+" tab for customization** — surfaced directly in the tab row rather than buried in settings. Lowers friction for personalization.
- **Inline "New" pill badge on tab label** — signals new content type without disrupting the tab bar's visual rhythm. More elegant than a notification dot.
- **Publication + author attribution pattern** — "In [Publication] by [Author]" — gives two layers of context in one compact line, useful for platforms with nested content hierarchies.
- **Negative action in metadata row** — the minus/block button lives in the same row as engagement stats, not hidden in a long-press or swipe. Surfaces content control without making it prominent.
- **Sparkle indicator on profile avatar in nav** — subtle animated-feeling badge that signals something new without a hard red dot. Softer, less alarming signal.
- **FAB for compose** — unusual on a feed screen; implies reading and writing are equal first-class actions.

---

## Notes
- Open divider layout > card layout for text-heavy content feeds
- Gold star for "boosted/featured" = warm, non-intrusive quality signal
- "In Publication by Author" — steal this attribution pattern for multi-author platforms
- Inline pill badge on tabs is cleaner than notification dots
- Minus button in-row = content control without hiding it
- Sparkle on avatar = soft "something's new" signal, avoid red dots where possible
- FAB on feed = commitment to creator-first positioning
- Two accent colors max, each with one job

## Screen
Single post detail/thread view in a social microblogging app. Sits one level deep from a feed — user tapped into a specific post to see its replies and engagement. Layout is vertical stack: nav bar → original post → contextual banner → reply thread → composer input → tab bar.

## Layout & Spacing
Original post gets generous vertical breathing room — timestamp and view count sit on their own line below the post body, giving it editorial weight. Clear visual hierarchy separates the "parent" post from replies via a subtle horizontal divider. The contextual share banner is inset with light background fill, creating a contained card within the scroll flow. Reply items are more compact/dense than the parent post, reinforcing the hierarchy. Bottom composer sits flush above the tab bar, anchored persistently.

## Components
- **Nav bar**: Back arrow left, centered bold label, overflow (···) right — minimal, no color
- **Author row**: Circular avatar + display name + handle inline; platform logo mark floated far right as a subtle branding/source tag
- **Post body**: Large-ish plain text, no container/card — floats directly on white
- **Metadata row**: Timestamp · date · view count in muted small text, dot-separated
- **Action bar (parent)**: 5 icons evenly spaced — comment (with count), repost, like, bookmark, share. Outlined/stroke style icons
- **Contextual banner**: Light blue-tinted card, upload icon + descriptive text + dismiss X. Soft rounded corners, no hard border
- **Reply row**: Compact — avatar, name, handle, timestamp, mute icon, overflow all on one line; reply text below; own action bar with analytics icon (bar chart) added
- **Composer input**: Full-width pill/rounded rect with placeholder text, very low visual weight
- **Tab bar**: 5 icons, active state shown with small blue dot on home icon; notification bell has blue badge with count

## Typography & Color
- Display name: medium-weight ~16px, black
- Handle/metadata: regular, ~13px, medium gray
- Post body: regular ~17–18px, black — largest text on screen, draws eye immediately
- Timestamp line: ~13px, gray — clearly tertiary
- Banner text: ~14px, dark gray, regular weight
- **Color palette**: Near-monochrome — black, white, multiple grays. Single accent: blue used exclusively for interactive signals (dot on active tab, notification badge, banner background tint). No decorative color.

## Patterns
- **View count as social proof metric** displayed inline with timestamp — treats views as a lightweight engagement signal without inflating importance
- **Contextual education banner mid-feed**: Rather than a modal or tooltip, the share prompt is embedded inline as a dismissible card between the post and its replies — non-blocking, contextually relevant placement
- **Analytics icon in reply action bar** (bar chart) but absent from parent post action bar — subtle role differentiation: reply authors get analytics access, reinforcing the "your content" ownership model
- **Platform logo as source attribution** floated top-right of parent post — functions as both branding and content origin marker, unusual placement that doesn't interfere with content
- **Persistent composer anchored above tab bar** — keeps reply action always accessible without a FAB, reduces tap distance
- **Mute icon directly in reply header row** — surfaces a moderation action at the content level rather than hiding it behind overflow, prioritizing control for the post author viewing replies
- **Active tab indicator as micro-dot** rather than filled icon or underline — extremely minimal active state

## Notes
- Inline dismissible banners > modals for contextual education
- View count beside timestamp = low-friction metric display, no dedicated section needed
- Role-based action bars (different icons for own post vs. reply) — smart contextual tooling
- Blue used ONLY for system signals (active, unread) — zero decorative use = high signal clarity
- Mute surfaced at reply level = author-first moderation UX
- Persistent composer above tab bar = no FAB needed in thread views
- Platform logo top-right as subtle provenance mark — worth stealing for multi-source feed apps

## Screen
AI conversational assistant home screen — the entry/discovery point where users choose how to engage. Two-column card grid dominates the layout, with a persistent chat input bar anchored at the bottom. This is a "mode selection" or "conversation starter" screen sitting at the top of the app hierarchy.

---

## Layout & Spacing
Generous warm cream background creates breathing room. Two-column grid of equal-width cards with consistent gutters (~12px). Cards are tall rectangles — image occupying roughly 55% top, label text in the bottom 45%. Bottom chat bar floats above content, slightly inset from screen edges with rounded pill shape. Page title sits high-left with profile icon top-right. Cards intentionally bleed off-screen bottom, signaling scrollability.

---

## Components
- **Welcome header** — Large serif/display weight, left-aligned, dark forest green
- **Profile button** — Circular icon button, muted cream fill, top-right corner
- **Conversation mode cards** — Rounded rectangle (~16px radius), cream background, top-half illustration, bottom-half label text. No borders, subtle shadow implied by card lift
- **Illustrations** — Editorial-style gouache/watercolor paintings, not photography or flat icons — warm, humanistic, analog feeling
- **Chat input bar** — Pill-shaped, full-width minus margins, contains logo mark left, placeholder text center, phone/voice icon button right in teal circle

---

## Typography & Color
**Type hierarchy:**
- Title: Large display serif, ~32px, deep forest green
- Card labels: Medium weight serif, ~18–20px, same dark green, left-aligned, multi-line allowed

**Color palette:**
- Background: Warm off-white/cream (#F5F0E8 range)
- Text/UI: Deep forest green (~#2D4A3E)
- Accent: Muted teal on the voice CTA button
- Illustrations bring in terracotta, sage, warm browns — all desaturated/vintage-toned

Color used functionally: single accent (teal) reserved exclusively for the primary action button, making it the only true CTA on screen.

---

## Patterns
**Illustration as UX wayfinding** — Each card uses a distinct painted scene to emotionally communicate the mode before the user reads the label. The image does the heavy lifting; text confirms. This is rare and effective.

**Intentional editorial art style** — Gouache/watercolor illustrations signal warmth, humanity, and non-digital intimacy — directly countering the "cold AI" perception. Deliberate brand-through-art-direction pattern.

**Mode-first onboarding** — Rather than a blank chat input, the screen presents curated conversation intents. Reduces blank-canvas anxiety. Lowers barrier to first interaction.

**Scrollable grid implies abundance** — Cards cut off at bottom edge, communicating "more options exist" without requiring a "see more" button.

**Voice CTA elevated** — Phone icon in the chat bar elevates voice as equal to text, unusual for AI chat apps. Teal circle draws eye immediately.

**No iconography except profile** — All navigation/mode selection handled through illustrated cards, not icon+label tabs. Removes abstraction layer.

---

## Notes
- Painted illustrations > icons for emotional mode selection — steal this
- Cream bg + forest green = warm authority, not sterile tech
- Single accent color discipline — teal only on primary action
- Card = image (emotion) + label (confirmation) pattern
- Bleed cards off-screen = implicit scroll affordance, no "scroll for more" needed
- Voice parity with text in input bar — worth considering for any assistant UI
- Serif type choice signals thoughtfulness, not speed — intentional pacing cue
- No color-coded categories — unity over differentiation here

## Screen
Social discovery app — map-based people-finding screen. Full-screen map view showing nearby users with a persistent action bar at the bottom. Sits in the core "explore" flow after onboarding. The map is the entire UI canvas, not a component within a screen.

## Layout & Spacing
Full-bleed map occupies 100% of screen real estate. City/location label overlaid top-left in large bold type. Action bar floats at bottom as a pill-shaped element sitting on top of the map. No traditional header or nav chrome — everything lives on the map layer. The bottom action zone uses generous padding and a clear visual separation between the primary CTA and secondary action.

## Components
- **User avatar pins**: Circular cropped photos used as map pins — no traditional teardrop pin shape, just the face itself as the locator
- **Distance orbit rings**: Dotted circular rings radiating from the central user avatar with "22M" labels at multiple points around the circumference — distance shown as a radial annotation, not a badge
- **3D emoji/gift objects**: Large 3D-rendered objects scattered across the lower map (hearts, fire, poop, eyes, crying face, beer, hello badge) with small text labels beneath each — function as interactive map items/gifts
- **Primary CTA pill**: Wide golden-yellow gradient pill button with "Message" text — high contrast, warm color against cool map
- **Secondary action button**: White circular button with phone icon, right-aligned next to the primary pill
- **Distance compass widget**: Small circular widget bottom-left showing "7.2K mi" with compass tick marks — analog instrument aesthetic
- **Zoom indicator**: "4X" typographic overlay on map in heavy condensed type

## Typography & Color
- Location label: Extra-bold/black weight, large (~32pt), all-caps feel — dominates the top of screen
- "4X" zoom: Heavy condensed display type, used as a map overlay annotation
- Gift labels: Small caption text beneath 3D objects, casual/lowercase
- Distance labels: Small, tight, placed along orbit ring
- **Color palette**: Warm golden yellow for primary CTA (high energy), white for secondary button, map uses standard muted beige/tan tones, 3D objects introduce saturated color pops (red, orange, purple, brown). Overall feel is warm-toned.

## Patterns
- **Face-as-pin**: Using the actual user photo as the map marker removes abstraction — you're not tracking a dot, you're seeing a person. Dramatically more humanizing than icon pins.
- **Radial distance display**: Distance shown as orbit rings around the user rather than a static badge — communicates "you are the center, others orbit you" conceptually. The repeated "22M" around the ring reinforces precision from any reading angle.
- **3D objects as map content**: Gifts/reactions rendered as 3D objects placed geographically on the map — blurs the line between AR and 2D map, makes the map feel alive and playful rather than utilitarian
- **Floating split action bar**: Primary + secondary actions paired in a floating bar rather than full-width — keeps map visible, feels less intrusive
- **Zero chrome philosophy**: No cards, no panels, no drawers visible — all UI is either overlaid on the map or floated above it. Maximizes immersion.
- **Warm CTA against cool map**: The golden pill button creates instant visual hierarchy against the desaturated map background — no need for drop shadows or borders

## Notes
- Face pins > icon pins for social apps — try this pattern
- Orbit rings for distance = spatial metaphor that feels native to maps
- 3D objects on 2D maps = huge engagement hook, makes map feel like a game world
- "Zero chrome" map UI — overlay everything, panel nothing
- Warm yellow CTA on cool map = strong contrast without darkness
- Compass widget as distance display — analog instrument aesthetic adds personality
- City name as giant display type overlay = instant context, no header needed
- Split pill + circle button pairing is clean — avoids two equal-weight buttons

## Screen
Home/dashboard screen of a meditation and wellness app. Entry point after login — serves as the daily hub showing streak progress, promotional content, and content discovery. Layout flows vertically: full-bleed hero image at top → streak tracker overlay → weekly habit grid → promotional banner → content section → bottom nav.

## Layout & Spacing
The hero landscape photo takes roughly 40% of the screen height, creating an immersive mood-setting zone. Content below transitions from the image into a deep navy panel, creating a seamless visual continuation rather than a hard break. The streak ring sits precisely at the image/panel boundary, bridging both zones. Weekly day indicators are evenly spaced in a single row with generous tap targets. The promo banner has comfortable internal padding with a square icon container on the left, text block in the middle, and chevron right-aligned. Consistent horizontal margins (~16px) throughout the scrollable content area.

## Components
- **Full-bleed hero image** — edge-to-edge landscape photo with no overlay or scrim; sky bleeds into the navy background naturally
- **Circular streak ring** — donut-style progress ring in cyan/blue gradient with large numeral center and label beneath; sits on a dark circular base with subtle shadow
- **Weekly habit dots** — 7 small circles labeled M–F+weekend; completed days show white checkmarks on slightly brighter circles; today's circle has a visible ring/outline treatment; past incomplete days are muted/ghosted
- **Confirmation microcopy** — centered, two-line motivational text below the habit row
- **Promotional banner card** — rounded rectangle with dark navy fill, purple square icon container (gift icon), bold title, secondary descriptor text, right chevron
- **Bottom nav bar** — pill-shaped frosted/translucent container with 4 icons; active state (Home) has filled white icon + label; inactive states are dimmer

## Typography & Color
**Type hierarchy:** Logo in elegant script at top. Streak number is the largest text element (~36px bold). Section header "Popular on Calm" is medium-weight sans-serif. Banner title is bold white. Supporting text is small, regular weight, slightly muted white/light blue. Day labels are tiny caps.

**Color palette:**
- Deep navy blue (#1a2a4a range) — primary background
- Cyan/sky blue gradient — streak ring accent, active states
- Muted slate blue — inactive day dots, ghost states
- Purple/lavender — promotional icon background, adds warmth contrast
- White — primary text, active icons
- Full-color photography — hero zone only

Color is used functionally: cyan signals progress/achievement, purple signals commercial/promotional, muted tones signal inactive/past states.

## Patterns
- **Threshold placement of key widget** — the streak ring straddles the hero image and the content panel, making it feel anchored in the scene rather than just UI chrome; creates depth and visual interest
- **Natural color continuation** — the deep blue of the mountain lake photo transitions almost imperceptibly into the navy app background; no hard edge, no overlay needed
- **Graduated day-dot states** — three distinct visual states (past incomplete = dark muted, completed = checkmark bright, today = outlined ring) communicate temporal position without any labels
- **Goal-reached positive reinforcement copy** — the microcopy appears only after the goal threshold is met, making it feel earned rather than generic
- **Frosted pill nav bar** — the bottom nav uses a floating pill with translucency rather than a full-width bar, reducing visual weight at the bottom and maintaining the immersive feel
- **Promotional banner as content card** — the offer is styled identically to content cards (same rounding, same dark fill) so it feels native rather than like an ad interruption
- **Scenery customization affordance** — the landscape icon in the top-left implies the hero image is changeable, turning a static background into a personalization feature

## Notes
- Hero-to-UI color match = zero-cost immersion trick, worth stealing
- Streak ring at image boundary = "bridge element" pattern — use to connect hero zones to content
- Three-state habit dots: ghost / check / outlined-today — clean temporal encoding
- Pill nav floats = less visual weight, more premium feel
- Promo card styled as content = reduces ad blindness
- Positive microcopy gated behind goal completion = micro-delight moment
- Purple accent for commercial CTAs = separates monetization from core UX without being jarring

## Screen
Travel discovery app home screen — the entry point of the app experience. Serves as an exploratory feed combining search, location-based suggestions, and curated content sections. Layout flows vertically: header → search → location prompt → editorial card carousel → trending section (partially visible). Primary interactive elements: global search bar, location permission CTA, horizontally scrollable destination cards, heart/save buttons, bottom navigation.

## Layout & Spacing
Clean vertical rhythm with generous section padding between distinct content zones. The search bar gets significant breathing room above and below — treated as a primary action, not just a utility. The location prompt sits in its own contained row with clear left-to-right reading: icon → copy → chevron. Section headers ("Plan your next adventure") are left-aligned, bold, and sit flush before their carousels. Card carousel bleeds off the right edge, signaling horizontal scroll without any explicit indicator. Two distinct card sizes visible — larger editorial cards in the first carousel vs. smaller square cards in trending.

## Components
- **Search bar**: Full-width pill shape, light stroke border, left-aligned search icon, muted placeholder text. No fill color — white/transparent background.
- **Location prompt row**: Circular green icon container with a location-plus glyph, two-line text block (bold headline + muted subtext), trailing arrow chevron. Inline, non-modal permission request.
- **Destination cards (large)**: Full-bleed photography, rounded corners, city/country name in white bold type anchored bottom-left. Category tags (pill chips) top-left. Heart icon top-right in a white circle button. Gradient overlay implied at bottom for text legibility.
- **Category tag chips**: Small white rounded rectangles with dark text, sitting directly on the image.
- **Heart/save button**: White circular button, outline heart icon — consistent across both card sizes.
- **Notification bell**: Top-right header icon, minimal outline style.

## Typography & Color
Strong two-level hierarchy: bold section headers (~22px) vs. card destination names (large, ~28px bold white) vs. supporting subtext (muted gray, ~13px). The location prompt uses a medium-weight headline with a lighter secondary line beneath it — clear subordination. Color palette is restrained: white background, near-black text, medium gray for secondary text, and a single brand green used only for the location icon background. Green functions as an accent/action signal. White overlaid on photography for card text — relies on image contrast rather than a heavy scrim.

## Patterns
- **Inline permission request as a feature hook**: Rather than a system dialog, the location access prompt is embedded as a contextual card row with benefit-first copy ("Looking for something nearby?"). Reduces friction and frames the permission as a user benefit.
- **Category tags on imagery**: Floating pill labels on the card images act as content metadata without requiring a separate text area below the card — keeps cards compact and scannable.
- **Partial card bleed for scroll affordance**: The second card in the carousel is intentionally cropped, communicating horizontal scrollability without any scroll indicator UI. Elegant implicit signaling.
- **Save action embedded in browse**: Heart buttons are present during discovery, not just on detail pages — reduces the steps to capture intent at the moment of inspiration.
- **Two carousel densities**: Large immersive cards for editorial/curated content vs. smaller square cards for trending — visual hierarchy between "aspirational" and "popular" content types without needing labels to explain the difference.
- **Benefit-first microcopy**: "Plan your next adventure" and "Trending with travelers" are emotionally framed, not functional labels like "Destinations" or "Popular."

## Notes
- Inline permission prompt > modal interrupt — always frame as user benefit first
- Bleed crop = scroll hint, no arrows needed
- Two card sizes = implicit content hierarchy (editorial vs. social proof)
- Tags on image = metadata without layout cost
- Green accent used sparingly = high signal value when it appears
- Save-on-browse pattern — capture intent at discovery, not just detail
- White pill chips on photography — needs careful image selection to maintain legibility
- Section headers: conversational/aspirational tone over functional labels

## Screen
A multi-currency digital wallet home screen — fintech/money transfer app. This is the primary dashboard, the first screen after login. Layout flows top-to-bottom: header utility bar → horizontal wallet selector → action shortcuts → recently paid contacts → transaction history → bottom nav.

## Layout & Spacing
Content is divided into two clear visual zones separated by a subtle horizontal rule: the "active/functional" upper zone (wallets + actions) and the "historical/informational" lower zone (transactions). The wallet selector is a horizontally scrollable pill row — compact and non-intrusive. The action shortcuts use a horizontal card carousel with generous padding inside each tile. Section headers ("Recently paid," "Transactions") use a consistent label + "View all" pill pattern. Transaction rows use comfortable vertical rhythm with clear left-icon / center-text / right-amount three-column structure.

## Components
- **Wallet selector pills**: Rounded rectangle chips with flag emoji + currency code + balance. Selected state appears as black filled circle (app logo). "Add wallet" uses a + icon chip at the end of the row.
- **Action cards**: Square-ish rounded cards in bold distinct colors (green, yellow, purple). Each contains a 3D illustrative icon and a short label at the bottom-left. Partially visible third card signals horizontal scroll.
- **Recently paid avatars**: Circular avatar thumbnails in a horizontal row — blurred/redacted here but clearly a contact scroll strip.
- **Transaction rows**: Left-aligned icon (branded or avatar), merchant name + timestamp, right-aligned amount. Currency conversion transactions show two-line amounts (debit in black, converted credit in green).
- **"View all" pill**: Small rounded rectangle with light gray fill, minimal label — consistent across both sections.
- **Bottom nav**: 5-item tab bar with outline icons + labels. "Pay" uses a diagonal arrows icon suggesting transfer directionality.

## Typography & Color
- **Type hierarchy**: Section headers are bold ~18px; transaction merchant names are medium weight ~15px; timestamps and secondary info are light/regular ~13px in gray.
- **Amounts**: Right-aligned, medium-bold. Negative amounts in near-black, positive amounts in green — classic debit/credit color coding.
- **Palette**: White background, near-black text. Accent colors confined to the action cards (vivid green, yellow-green, purple). Green used functionally for positive transactions. Gray used for secondary text and UI chrome.
- **Color is purposeful**: No decorative color in the transaction list — color only appears where it carries meaning (positive = green, action cards = category-coded).

## Patterns
- **Partial card reveal as scroll affordance**: The third action card is deliberately cropped, signaling horizontal scrollability without any explicit indicator — clean and implicit.
- **Dual-amount transaction row**: Currency conversion shows both the deducted amount and the received amount in a different currency stacked — solves a genuinely complex data display problem elegantly in one row.
- **Wallet selector as persistent context**: The currency pills stay visible above the fold, keeping the user oriented about which wallet is active without requiring a separate screen.
- **3D illustration in action cards**: Using isometric/3D objects in the shortcut cards gives them visual weight and memorability compared to flat icon buttons — makes actions feel more tangible.
- **Blurred "Recently paid" section**: Privacy-first pattern — contacts are obscured by default, reducing shoulder-surfing exposure without hiding the feature's existence.
- **"Get help" as top-right text link**: Avoids burying support behind a hamburger menu — surfaces it at the header level, suggesting the app expects users may need help frequently (common in fintech onboarding contexts).

## Notes
- Dual-currency transaction row = killer pattern for any FX/conversion product
- Partial card crop = free scroll affordance, no arrows needed
- Keep action cards illustrative not iconographic — more tactile, more memorable
- Wallet chips as persistent balance display = no need to navigate to see balance
- Privacy blur on contacts = good default, consider toggle
- "View all" pill is a great alternative to text links for section expansion — feels tappable
- Color reserved for meaning only in data rows — restraint = clarity
- 3-column transaction layout (icon / description+time / amount) is the gold standard for finance lists
